skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Connect Claude to Pexo, an AI video creation platform. Describe a video idea in natural language and this agent handles project creation, file uploads, message relay, polling, and delivery of finished short-form videos.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that turns natural language descriptions into short videos via the Pexo platform.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 534 static findings across 13 files (2110 lines). All findings are false positives from legitimate shell scripting patterns (command substitution in bash scripts), documentation examples (shell backticks in markdown code blocks), and expected API client behavior (API credential access, network requests to pexo.ai, config file in ~/.pexo/). The tool is a legitimate CLI wrapper for the Pexo AI video API. No malicious intent, obfuscation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. The heuristic dangerous combination finding is accurate in pattern but expected for an API client tool.

low_findings.title: Heuristic dangerous combination: code execution, network, and credentials
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the combination of shell script execution, API network requests, and API key access as a potentially dangerous pattern. After evaluation, this is a false positive: the skill is a legitimate API client that necessarily requires all three capabilities to function as a video creation agent. All network requests go to pexo.ai, credentials are handled via standard Authorization headers, and script execution is standard bash scripting with no command injection vectors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Source code review confirms legitimate API client pattern with no obfuscation, data exfiltration, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External commands in documentation files
low_findings.description: 349 instances of shell backtick/command substitution patterns flagged in markdown documentation files (SKILL.md, SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) and shell scripts. All are false positives: markdown instances are code block examples within fenced code blocks, not executable code. Shell script instances use standard $(cmd) substitution for legitimate purposes (curl calls to pexo.ai API, jq JSON processing, file operations). No command injection vectors present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown backticks are formatting characters for code blocks, not shell execution. Shell command substitutions are standard bash for legitimate API calls.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API credentials
low_findings.description: 25 instances of env_access detected for PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL. These are required configuration values for the API client. The skill documents these requirements in its metadata (requires.env). Credentials are used only for Authorization headers in API calls to pexo.ai. No credential logging or exfiltration found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Credential access is necessary and expected for an API client. Pattern is consistent with standard Authorization header usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell scripts with command substitution and network access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses bash scripts that execute commands, make HTTP requests to pexo.ai, and access API credentials. This is expected behavior for a CLI API client tool. All arguments to curl and jq are hardcoded or derived from script parameters without injection vectors. Temp files created with mktemp are properly cleaned up with rm -f. No dangerous patterns confirmed after manual review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Full source review confirms standard bash scripting practices with proper cleanup and no injection vulnerabilities.

content.user_title: Create AI Videos with Pexo Agent
content.value_statement: Describe a video idea in natural language and this agent connects Claude to the Pexo AI video platform to produce the final result. It handles project creation, file uploads, message relay with the video engine, and delivery of finished videos.
actual_capabilities: Create short AI videos from 5 to 120 seconds from a text description
Upload images, videos, and audio files as visual or audio references
Monitor video production progress and get notified when finished
Request video revisions through natural language feedback
Preview and select from multiple video variants during production
limitations: Requires a Pexo API key and active account with credits
Video production takes 15 to 20 minutes minimum
Limited to short-form videos between 5 and 120 seconds
Cannot directly edit the final video file, only request new revisions

use_cases.title: Marketing Product Videos
use_cases.description: Create short product advertisements and promotional videos for social media campaigns. Describe your product and the agent handles the rest.

use_cases.title: Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Produce TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts content quickly. Upload reference images and describe the style you want.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Brand and Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: Create brand introduction videos or product explainers without video editing skills. Provide your brand assets and key messages as input.
use_cases.target_user: Small business owners and startup founders

prompt_templates.title: Simple video from description
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product video for my new running shoes.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a basic product video with no specific references.

prompt_templates.title: Video with uploaded reference images
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a brand introduction video using our company logo and tagline.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user uploads their logo and tagline as reference files.

prompt_templates.title: Video with multiple file types
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a tutorial-style video about how to use our mobile app, using screenshots I will upload.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user uploads screenshots as visual references for a tutorial.

prompt_templates.title: Revision request on existing video
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise the last video to be more upbeat with faster pacing and brighter colors.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to revise a video that was already produced.

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second ad for my coffee shop
output_examples.output: A short video ad showing coffee preparation shots, the shop interior ambiance, and text overlay with the coffee shop name and location. Duration is 15 seconds.

output_examples.input: Make an Instagram reel showcasing our summer collection
output_examples.output: A vertical 9:16 video with product shots transitioning to background music, with the brand's summer collection items displayed in sequence.

output_examples.input: Create a product demo for our new app
output_examples.output: A screen-focused video demonstrating the app's main features with voiceover, ending with a call-to-action and download link.

best_practices: Provide clear, specific descriptions of your video idea using natural language
Upload reference images to help Pexo understand your brand and visual style
Wait for the full production cycle to complete before requesting changes
anti_patterns: Adding technical video parameters like duration or aspect ratio that Pexo handles automatically
Sending multiple revision requests before receiving and reviewing the first result
Using web URLs directly for file uploads without downloading them first

faq.question: What types of videos can Pexo create?
faq.answer: Pexo creates short-form videos from 5 to 120 seconds, including product ads, social media clips, brand videos, and explainers.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: Video production typically takes 15 to 20 minutes for a 15-second video. Longer and more complex videos may take more time.

faq.question: Do I need a Pexo account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Pexo API key. Sign up at pexo.ai, create an API key in your account settings, and configure it in the Pexo config file.

faq.question: What file formats can I upload as references?
faq.answer: You can upload images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), and audio (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: Can I request changes to a finished video?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide feedback on the finished video and Pexo will revise it. Use the same project and describe what you want changed.

faq.question: Can I preview the video before it is finalized?
faq.answer: Yes, Pexo may present preview options during production. You can review these and select your preferred version.
